Defendant Paul J. Wagstaffes Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidentiary Issue And/Or Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff John Marshall
Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DAVID E. SMITH, Esq. of Smith Zitano Law Firm, LLP (Tel: 916-333-5933) for Plaintiff John Marshall;
Atty. MIA ROSENFELD, Esq. of Hansen, Kohls, Sommer & Jacob, LLP (Tel: 916-781-2550) for Defendant Paul J. Wagstaffe

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT PAUL WAGSTAFFE's Motion For Evidentiary Issue And/Or Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff John Marshall.

All parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for parties authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court and counsel discuss the parties' request for clarification. Matter submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the sole modification of correcting two instances where the word "Plaintiff" was used to replace them with the word "Defendant."

The Court rules as follows: Defendant's Motion For Evidentiary Issue and/or Terminating Sanctions is DENIED without prejudice. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Terminating sanctions are to be used sparingly, only when a court concludes that lesser sanctions would not bring about the compliance of the offending party. Here, Plaintiff has substantially (albeit untimely) complied. Even if he had not, the record before the Court would not justify terminating sanctions. Likewise, and in light of the policy favoring trial on the merits, the Court does not find that the requested evidence sanction-prohibiting any evidence of plaintiff's income loss-is warranted. Because Defendant did not produce a 2010 Marshall & Co. S Corp tax return, however, the Court denies the motion without prejudice to Plaintiff bringing a new motion for evidence sanctions specific to the failure to produce that return. This order is also without prejudice to Defendant bringing a motion for monetary sanctions for misuse of the discovery process under Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.030(a).

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at mpritt@bsfllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel John Marshalls Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PAUL WAGSTAFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL JOHN MARSHALL'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

No appearances. The Court takes the matter off calendar per moving party's notice filed March 5, 2020. Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff John Marshalls Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PAUL WAGSTAFFE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF JOHN MARSHALL'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

No appearances. Those who have not made an appearance are deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. As requested by Defendant, Plaintiff is ordered to provide further responses and produce responsive documents within 60 days of the date of this Order. Defendant's Motion is denied to the extent he seeks sanctions. Section 2023.040 provides in part: "A request for a sanction shall, in the notice of motion, identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought, and specify the type of sanction sought." Here, only the motion includes that information. That does not satisfy section 2023.040. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice of the Complaint filed in this matter is GRANTED. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
